title: Digital Self‑Sovereignty: Why Owning Your Data Matters
relevance: Explores the strategic and ethical reasons for moving content off platform silos, directly supporting the article’s argument for a personal site + RSS workflow.

heading: Mass‑Deleting Tweets as a Signal of Data Ownership
body: Removing historic tweets does more than clean a timeline; it signals a break with the platform’s implicit claim to perpetual custody of your voice. By erasing the record, you force a migration to a self‑hosted archive, where you control licensing, backup, and future distribution—key tenets of the Grabbit universal‑feed vision.

title: Building a Personal Content Pipeline
description: A step‑by‑step deep dive into creating a resilient publishing workflow: choosing a static‑site generator, setting up RSS feeds, automating cross‑post to social channels, and preserving metadata. Includes tool recommendations, security considerations, and migration scripts for legacy tweets.
